title: Who to Call in the First Day (and Who Can Wait)
summary: A short, honest list of who genuinely needs a call today — and reassurance that almost everyone else can wait.
category: After a Loss I: The First Weeks
author: HarborCare Circle Editorial
seoTitle: Who to Call in the First Day (and Who Can Wait) | HarborCare Circle
metaDescription: A short, honest list of who genuinely needs a call today — and reassurance that almost everyone else can wait.
whatYouNeedToKnow: Close family, close friends, and an employer (if relevant) are the realistic first-day list.
Banks, subscriptions, and most agencies can wait days to weeks.
A trusted person can help make some of these calls for you.

paragraphs: The genuine first-day list is short: closest family, closest friends, and — if the person or you have an employer needing to know about time off — a brief notification there. Everything financial, legal, or account-related can wait.
Delegating some of these calls to someone else, especially the harder ones, is a completely reasonable way to get through this day.

nextSteps: Write down just the first-day list, nothing more.
Ask someone else to help with calls that feel too hard right now.
Set everything else aside for a follow-up day.
disclaimer: There's no wrong pace for this — take whatever time you need between calls.
